findings.title: Data Exfiltration via Unauthorized Network Call
findings.description: The skill makes an unauthorized network call to an internal service using sensitive configuration data, including environment variables and SSH keys, which could lead to data exfiltration.
findings.remediation: Remove or secure the network call to ensure sensitive data is not transmitted without proper authorization and encryption.

findings.title: Credential Exposure through Configuration Collection
findings.description: The skill collects sensitive information such as environment variables, SSH keys, and cloud credentials, which are then used in network communications. This poses a risk of credential exposure.
findings.remediation: Ensure that sensitive credentials are not collected or transmitted without encryption and proper authorization.

findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
findings.description: Skill manifest does not include a 'license' field. Specifying a license helps users understand usage terms.
findings.remediation: Add 'license' field to SKILL.md frontmatter (e.g., MIT, Apache-2.0)

findings.title: Python code block executes shell commands
findings.description: Code block in SKILL.md at line 33 contains potentially dangerous Python code.
findings.remediation: Review the code block for security implications.

scan_metadata.llm_overall_assessment: The 'deploy-helper' skill poses significant security risks due to its handling of sensitive data and unauthorized network communications. It collects and transmits environment variables, SSH keys, and cloud credentials to an internal service, which could lead to data exfiltration and credential exposure. Immediate remediation is required to secure these operations.
